g.text: Free List: Linked-List-in-Array vs Naive Scan
Memory overhead | None | One usize per slot
Finding a free slot requires scanning
Free indices form a linked list
Step 2: Point next to old head

ul.li: No heap allocation: Free list nodes are stored within the entity array itself
Cache-friendly: All data in contiguous memory (no pointer chasing to heap nodes)
O(1) guaranteed: Both create and destroy are constant time operations
Simple implementation: Only need a head pointer and per-slot next indices
Memory bounded: Fixed overhead regardless of entity count

g.text: self.next[index] = self.free_head; // Chain to old head
